The Attachment Code
The Forgotten One Journal
Understanding Your Pattern

Nobody asked how you were doing today. This space is for you — the one who kept showing up for everyone else, and is only just beginning to remember they matter too.

What this journal is

A private space to find yourself again — not to fix yourself, but to finally remember who you were before you made yourself smaller to keep the peace. Every entry lives only on your device.

Speak or write

Every response field has a microphone button. Tap it to speak your answer — your words transcribe in real time. Speaking often reveals more than writing. Both are valid.

Private by design

Your entries are saved locally on this device only. No server. No database. No one but you can access what you write here.

Choose Your Portal
The Disappearing Act
Erosion · Self-Loss
How you stopped being a person and became a support system.
The Hypervigilance
Scanning · Managing
The constant reading of the room, managing the mood, bracing for the shift.
The Invisible Needs
Silence · Self-Abandonment
The wants and feelings you stopped having permission to voice.
The Resentment
Anger · Grief
The anger that built quietly while you kept being the understanding one.
The Original You
Identity · Before
Who you were before the relationship rewired your sense of self.
The Return to Self
Reclamation · Becoming
Finding your way back — not to who you were, but to who you actually are.

Daily Practice

Seven steps. Work through them in order, or drop into wherever feels most true today.

1
Arrival
Before your mind — check in with your body. Where are you on the spectrum right now? Pulled toward connection or pulled toward distance?
ContractedActivatedNumbRestlessOpenTenderGuardedReachingSafeUnsettled
2
The Story
About someone you love. About yourself. About what's about to happen. What narrative is running in the background right now?
3
The Evidence
Not the feeling — the verifiable fact. What has actually happened, without interpretation? What is the evidence for it — and what is the evidence against it?
4
The Origin
Where have you felt this before? How far back does this particular fear go? Where did you first learn to tell yourself this story?
5
The Need
Not what it's demanding. Not what it's doing. What does it actually need — underneath the behaviour?
6
The Choice
One small, conscious choice about how you respond — instead of reacting from the pattern. Not a grand gesture. Just one thing.
7
The Closing
Not performance. Not toxic positivity. One honest thing — however small — that you're willing to see about yourself today.

Private by design. Your entries are saved locally on this device only. No one else can access them.

Guided Prompts

Tell the guide what you're working through. She'll create personalised prompts, practices, or exercises written specifically for where you are right now — in this relationship, in this pattern, in this moment.

Understanding what triggered me
Examining whether my thoughts are real or assumed
Understanding how I lost myself
Understanding a pattern I keep repeating
Working through the hypervigilance
Moving toward repair
Connecting current patterns to earlier experiences
Making sense of the resentment I carry

this week

The pattern that showed up most

What was most present for you this week? The hypervigilance, the invisible needs, the resentment — which was loudest?

Something that shifted

What insight, however small, changed something? A moment you put yourself first, a moment you named a need, a moment you caught yourself scanning and chose to stop.

The moment I'm most proud of

One moment — however small — where you chose differently. Where the pattern didn't completely run the show.

This Week's Actions
Complete your daily 7-step spread
Notice one moment when you scan or manage someone else\'s mood — and pause
Name one need out loud today — to yourself, or to someone safe
Do one thing that is just for you, with no justification needed
Let yourself feel the resentment without immediately forgiving it away
Reclaim one small thing that belonged to the original you
Closing Ritual

You didn\'t lose yourself overnight. You won\'t find yourself overnight either. But every small act of returning — every need you voice, every space you take up, every moment you choose yourself — that is the work. You are allowed to come home to yourself. That\'s not selfish. That\'s survival.
